introSection: Thunderbolt was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was originally owned by George Moran. His son, Fred Moran, took over in 1965 when George died.

Fred Moran passed away on January 8, 1982. It was initially reported that Moran's partners would continue to operate the ride, but as of August the ride had reportedly not operated.

The ride remained standing but not operating until it was demolished on November 17, 2000.

The Thunderbolt roller coaster at Luna Park opened on the same plot of land in 2014.

fullText:
Thunderbolt was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, USA.

The ride was originally owned by George Moran. His son, Fred Moran, took over in 1965 when George died.

Fred Moran passed away on January 8, 1982. It was initially reported that Moran's partners would continue to operate the ride, but as of August the ride had reportedly not operated.

The ride remained standing but not operating until it was demolished on November 17, 2000.

The Thunderbolt roller coaster at Luna Park opened on the same plot of land in 2014.
